Scenario.overview: Communications Industry Simple Basic attackers from the 'Cutting Edge' campaign target communications firms using phishing to gain initial access, exploiting public-facing applications for execution, and causing impact through data destruction.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Employee Endpoints, Application Logs
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email, Web, Application Log Content

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers send phishing emails to employees, tricking them into clicking on malicious links or opening infected attachments, thus gaining entry into the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: After initial access, attackers exploit known vulnerabilities in public-facing applications such as web servers or VPNs to execute their payload.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Having established control, the attackers proceed to cause impact by destroying data, either by deletion or encryption, aiming to disrupt operations.

Scenario.summary: The Cutting Edge campaign employs basic yet effective techniques against communication firms, starting with phishing to gain access, moving to exploit vulnerable applications for execution, and culminating in data destruction to achieve their goals.
